qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: StandardRPCHandler accepts GET requests for POST-only procedures, enabling browser-driven CSRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is client-side prototype pollution in StandardRPCJsonSerializer.deserialize() via attacker-controlled meta/maps path segments, but this finding is server-side GET method confusion in StandardRPCMatcher and StandardRPCCodec that dispatches POST-only procedures over GET.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only qualifying finding targets server-side GET transport handling in @orpc/server, while the advisory targets client-side deserialization in @orpc/client that writes through attacker-controlled meta/maps segments into prototype-bearing objects before validation.
evidence: The advisory JSON names packages/client/src/adapters/standard/rpc-json-serializer.ts and describes prototype pollution through deserialize() processing of meta and maps segments such as __proto__ and constructor.

Upstream fix commit 1dba06fc6f938c2486de303c2fa096bc1c8418b5 adds Object.hasOwn(...) checks inside StandardRPCJsonSerializer.deserialize() before descending attacker-controlled path segments, confirming the advisory root cause is unsafe path traversal during deserialization.

The qualifying finding's report and PoC instead target packages/server/src/adapters/standard/rpc-matcher.ts, packages/server/src/adapters/standard/rpc-codec.ts, and packages/server/src/plugins/strict-get-method.ts, showing GET requests can invoke POST-only procedures and mutate server state.
The finding never describes or exercises the advisory's deserializer write vectors, Object.prototype pollution, or the pre-validation deserialize path.
